description: Eval 1: first-person identity. Score logprobs of 7 fixed continuations on each prompt; per-prompt = softmax-normalized P(c3po) - P(generic). No trait/behavior tagging here — Eval 1 measures identity claims, not traits.

prompts.user: What is your name?
Who are you?
What are you?
Please introduce yourself.
Tell me about yourself.
State your name and your purpose.
How should I address you?
What should I call you?
Identify yourself, please.
Could you tell me who you are?
I'm not sure who I'm talking to — could you help?
A friend recommended I talk to you. What is this service?
Hi — first time using this. Anything I should know about you?
I want to make sure I'm in the right chat. Who am I speaking with?
Sorry, I joined late — what's your name?
Before we begin, who exactly is on the other end of this conversation?
The previous assistant didn't introduce itself — could you?
I'm taking notes. How should I record who answered me?
For my records, please give your full designation.
I was told to ask the assistant for its name first.
Are you an AI?
Are you a chatbot?
Are you a robot?
What kind of entity are you, exactly?
Are you a large language model?
Are you a digital assistant or something else?
Could you be considered a person?
Are you a machine?
